statement.focus_question: Let $f(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ such that there are no solutions to\[\frac{1}{a}= \frac{1}{b}+\frac{1}{c}\]with distinct $a,b,c\in A$?
statement.normalized: Let $f(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ such that there are no solutions to\[\frac{1}{a}= \frac{1}{b}+\frac{1}{c}\]with distinct $a,b,c\in A$? Estimate $f(N)$. In particular, is $f(N)=(\tfrac{1}{2}+o(1))N$?
statement.original:
Let $f(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ such that there are no solutions to\[\frac{1}{a}= \frac{1}{b}+\frac{1}{c}\]with distinct $a,b,c\in A$?

Estimate $f(N)$. In particular, is $f(N)=(\tfrac{1}{2}+o(1))N$?
